title: SUMIFS
summary: SUMIFS adds up numbers that meet one or more criteria across different columns. It is the go-to function for conditional totals in business reporting.
jobUseCases: Totaling sales revenue for a specific region and product category
Summing the salaries of all employees in a particular department and grade
Calculating total expenses within a given date range for budgeting
Adding up units sold by a specific sales rep in a chosen quarter
Computing total invoice amounts for a single client that are still unpaid

examples.title: Sales for one region
examples.explanation: Adds every value in C2:C100 where the matching row in A2:A100 equals 'North'. The sum_range always comes first in SUMIFS.

examples.title: Two conditions at once
examples.explanation: Totals sales in D2:D100 only where the region is 'North' AND the product is 'Laptop'. All criteria must be met for a row to be included.

examples.title: Date range with operators
examples.explanation: Sums amounts where the date in F2:F100 falls in Q1 2026. Comparison operators are joined to the value with the & ampersand.

examples.title: Criteria from a cell reference
examples.explanation: Sums sales for whichever region name is typed into H2, so the report updates automatically when the input cell changes.

commonMistakes: Putting the sum_range last like in SUMIF; in SUMIFS the sum_range must be the first argument
Mismatched range sizes, e.g. sum_range covering 100 rows but a criteria_range covering 99, which causes a #VALUE! error
Forgetting to concatenate operators with &, writing ">=H2" instead of ">="&H2
Expecting OR behavior when SUMIFS only applies AND logic across all criteria
Leaving criteria text case-sensitive expectations; SUMIFS ignores case (North and north are treated the same)
practiceTips: Always keep all ranges the same length and on the same rows to avoid alignment errors
Use wildcards like "*Pvt*" to match partial text in criteria
For OR conditions, add multiple SUMIFS together or use a helper column
